page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is a compute-focused virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 360 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 3.75 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be handled via network-attached options.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores falling into the top 25 percent. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), which contribute to top-tier performance in cryptographic operations and image processing. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core results placing in the middle 50 percent, representing a tradeoff between massive parallel capacity and individual thread speed. Memory performance is similarly bifurcated, showing strong read and write bandwidth in large block tests but an average overall PassMark memory score.

This instance is highly capable for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and text processing, where it achieves top-tier benchmark results. It also demonstrates strong efficiency in CPU-based machine learning tasks, particularly for prompt processing on medium and large language models. Given its high density of dedicated vCPUs and advanced instruction sets, the c4-standard-96 offers a cost-efficient profile for parallelized enterprise workloads and compute-heavy microservices that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is an x86_64 compute-focused instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 360 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 48 physical cores. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies entirely on network-attached resources. The instance delivers strong multi-core performance, particularly in cryptographic execution, multi-threaded decompression, and image processing, supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. While single-core performance and overall memory scores are average, its high parallel processing capacity makes it highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads. It is well-suited for software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is an x86_64 instance with 96 vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU. It offers strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 features 96 vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-core x86_64 compute instance featuring advanced matrix extensions for parallel processing, multi-threaded workloads, and CPU-based inference.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 360 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and matrix math
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration
Top-tier throughput for static web serving and multi-threaded decompression
